zh.lifeSummary: Jan Vilain van Gent是Jan Vilain van Gent和Margaretha Arnoud van Gavere的兒子，於1394年出生。他和Goedele Godfried Raas van Zemst至少有2個女兒。他去世於1449年6月8日在比利時安特維爾次維德雷特享年55歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Jan Vilain van Gent是Jan Vilain van Gent和Margaretha Arnoud van Gavere的儿子，于1394年出生。他和Goedele Godfried Raas van Zemst至少有2个女儿。他去世于1449年6月8日在比利时安特維爾次維德雷特享年55岁。

nameMeanings.name: Vilain
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English:  see  Villain  .

variants.name: Villain
meanings: Norman, English: status name from Anglo-Norman French villein, vilein ‘serf, bondman, servile tenant’, denoting an occupier or cultivator of land who was entirely subject to a lord or was attached to a manor.

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Gent
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern) and French: nickname from Old French gent, Middle English gent(e) ‘high born, noble’, hence ‘noble of character or conduct, courteous’, also ‘nice, friendly’.
English: habitational name from Ghent (Gent in Dutch), a city in Flanders, Belgium. Compare Ghent .
German: from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name formed with gand ‘spell’, such as Gandolf.

variants.name: Jent
meanings: English: variant of   Gent  .

variants.name: Ghent
meanings: English: habitational name from Ghent (Gent in Dutch), a city in Flanders, Belgium. Compare Gent .

variants.name: Gentes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Henri, Lucien, Marcel, Normand.
French (Gentès): probably a nickname derived from Old French gent ‘high born, noble’, also ‘nice, friendly’.
German: patronymic from the personal name   Gent  .

variants.name: Genther
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Otto.
German:
variant of   Gent  .

variants.name: Genthe
meanings: German: variant of Gente (see Gent ), or a habitational name from Gentha in Saxony-Anhalt, east of Wittenberg.

nameMeanings.name: Jan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

en.lifeSummary: Jan Vilain van Gent was born in 1394 as the son of Jan Vilain van Gent and Margaretha Arnoud van Gavere. He had at least 2 daughters with Goedele Godfried Raas van Zemst. He died on 8 June 1449, in Zwijndrecht, Antwerp, Belgium, at the age of 55.

fr.lifeSummary: Jan Vilain van Gent est né en 1394, fils de Jan Vilain van Gent et Margaretha Arnoud van Gavere. Il a eu au moins 2 filles avec Goedele Godfried Raas van Zemst. Il est décédé le 8 juin 1449, à Zwijndrecht, Anvers, Belgique, à l’âge de 55 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Jan Vilain van Gent wurde 1394, als Sohn von Jan Vilain van Gent und Margaretha Arnoud van Gavere geboren. Er hatte mit Goedele Godfried Raas van Zemst mindestens 2 Töchter. Er starb am 8. Juni 1449 im Alter von 55 Jahren in Zwijndrecht, Antwerpen, Belgien.

it.lifeSummary: Jan Vilain van Gent è nato il nel 1394 come figlio di Jan Vilain van Gent e Margaretha Arnoud van Gavere. Lui avuto almeno 2 figlie con Goedele Godfried Raas van Zemst. Morì il 8 giugno 1449, nel Zwijndrecht, Anversa, Belgio, all'età di 55 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Jan Vilain van Gentは1394年にJan Vilain van GentとMargaretha Arnoud van Gavereの息子として生まれた。彼はGoedele Godfried Raas van Zemstとの間に少なくとも2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1449年6月8日にベルギーアントウエルペンズウィジンドレチトで55歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jan Vilain van Gent(은)는 1394년 Jan Vilain van Gent(와)과 Margaretha Arnoud van Gavere의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Goedele Godfried Raas van Zemst(와)과 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1449년 6월 8일 벨기에 앤트워펜 즈위제이드레트에서 55세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Jan Vilain van Gent nasceu em 1394 filho de Jan Vilain van Gent e Margaretha Arnoud van Gavere. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhas com Goedele Godfried Raas van Zemst. Ele faleceu em 8 de junho de 1449, em Zwijndrecht, Antuérpia, Bélgica, com 55 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Jan Vilain van Gent родился в 1394 г. Его родителей звали Jan Vilain van Gent и Margaretha Arnoud van Gavere. У него было по крайней мере 2 дочери от Goedele Godfried Raas van Zemst. Он умер 8 июня 1449 г. в местности звиджндречт, антвирпен, Бельгия в возрасте 55 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Jan Vilain van Gent nació en 1394 como hijo de Jan Vilain van Gent y Margaretha Arnoud van Gavere. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijas con Goedele Godfried Raas van Zemst. Murió el 8 de junio de 1449, en Zwijndrecht, Amberes, Bélgica, a la edad de 55 años.
